_comment: Curated Malaysia-national doorstep issues salient to the 2026 Johor vote — same discipline as issues.json: dated, receipted, sourced, verdict-flagged. VERIFICATION METHOD DISCLOSURE: this environment's egress blocks direct page loads of news/gov sites, so every fact was corroborated via multiple independent search-result excerpts tied to the cited URLs (finder pass + a second independent search pass), not full on-page reads — hence PARTLY_CONFIRMED rather than VERIFIED. Re-verify against the live pages before printing campaign material. Each issue's `theme` maps to a muda_stances.json theme so the MUDA angle can be overlaid in the muda edition.

issues.issue_bm: Subsidi petrol kini bersasar: RM1.99 seliter dengan MyKad sejak 30 September 2025 — tetapi kuota bulanan dipotong daripada 300 kepada 200 liter pada 1 April 2026, dan harga pasaran RON95 tanpa subsidi mencecah RM3.37 seliter pada minggu kempen ini, sementara BUDI Diesel RM2.10 yang bermula 1 Julai berkongsi kuota 200 liter yang sama.
issues.issue_en: Petrol subsidy is now targeted: RM1.99 a litre with MyKad since 30 September 2025 — but the monthly quota was cut from 300 to 200 litres on 1 April 2026, the unsubsidised market price of RON95 hit RM3.37 a litre in this campaign week, and the new BUDI Diesel RM2.10 scheme that started 1 July shares the same 200-litre quota.
issues.receipt_bm: MOF (29 Sep 2025): BUDI95 bermula penuh 30 Sep — RM1.99 seliter melalui pengesahan MyKad, harga tanpa subsidi RM2.60 sebelum apungan mingguan. The Star (26 Mac 2026): subsidi BUDI95+BUDI Diesel ~RM4 bilion sebulan selepas minyak mentah melepasi USD100; PM umum kuota dipotong 300→200 liter mulai 1 April. MOF (22 Jun 2026): BUDI Madani Diesel RM2.10 seliter seluruh negara mulai 1 Julai 2026, kuota 200 liter dikongsi dengan BUDI95. paultan.org (30 Jun 2026): harga minggu 1-8 Julai — RON95 tanpa subsidi RM3.37, RON97 RM4.00, diesel RM3.97.
issues.receipt_en: MOF (29 Sep 2025): BUDI95 full rollout 30 Sep — RM1.99/litre via MyKad verification, unsubsidised price RM2.60 before moving to a weekly float. The Star (26 Mar 2026): BUDI95+BUDI Diesel subsidies hit ~RM4 billion a month after crude passed USD100; the PM announced the quota cut from 300 to 200 litres from 1 April. MOF (22 Jun 2026): BUDI Madani Diesel at RM2.10/litre nationwide from 1 July 2026, sharing the 200-litre quota with BUDI95. paultan.org (30 Jun 2026): 1-8 July week — unsubsidised RON95 RM3.37, RON97 RM4.00, diesel RM3.97.

issues.issue_bm: Sejak 1 Julai 2025 SST diperluas — cukai jualan 5-10% ke atas barangan bukan asas dan cukai perkhidmatan 6-8% ke atas sewaan, pembinaan, kewangan, penjagaan kesihatan dan pendidikan swasta — dijangka mengutip ~RM10 bilion setahun menjelang 2026; pada hari yang sama struktur tarif elektrik baharu berkuat kuasa, dan pelarasan bahan api (AFA) April 2026 menjadikan bil pengguna besar lebih mahal.
issues.issue_en: Since 1 July 2025 the SST has been expanded — 5-10% sales tax on non-essential goods and 6-8% service tax on rental, construction, financial services, private healthcare and private education — expected to raise ~RM10 billion a year by 2026; the same day a new electricity tariff structure took effect, and the April 2026 fuel adjustment (AFA) made larger users' bills more expensive.
issues.receipt_bm: Malay Mail (9 Jun 2025): skop dan kadar perluasan SST mulai 1 Julai (barangan asas kekal 0%). Malay Mail (27 Jun 2025): selepas bantahan awam — epal/oren/kurma dikecualikan, perkhidmatan kecantikan digugurkan, ambang pendaftaran sewaan/kewangan dinaikkan ke RM1 juta. Malay Mail (24 Jul 2025): MOF jangka tambahan RM5 bilion pada 2025 dan kira-kira dua kali ganda pada 2026. Bernama (20 Jun 2025): tarif asas purata Semenanjung 45.40 sen/kWj mulai 1 Julai 2025; pengguna domestik ≤1,000kWj sebulan tidak terjejas; AFA bulanan menggantikan ICPT. The Edge (Apr 2026): rebat AFA April 2026 mengecil ke 1.68 sen/kWj — contoh bil 800kWj naik dari ~RM328 ke ~RM342 sebulan.
issues.receipt_en: Malay Mail (9 Jun 2025): the SST expansion's scope and rates from 1 July (essentials stay zero-rated). Malay Mail (27 Jun 2025): after public backlash — apples/oranges/dates exempted, beauty services dropped, the rental/financial registration threshold raised to RM1 million. Malay Mail (24 Jul 2025): MOF expects an extra RM5 billion in 2025 and roughly double that in 2026. Bernama (20 Jun 2025): Peninsular average base tariff 45.40 sen/kWh from 1 July 2025; domestic users ≤1,000kWh/month unaffected; monthly AFA replaces ICPT. The Edge (Apr 2026): the April 2026 AFA rebate shrank to 1.68 sen/kWh — an 800kWh bill rising from ~RM328 to ~RM342 month-on-month.

issues.issue_bm: Gaji minimum RM1,700 berkuat kuasa 1 Februari 2025 dan diwajibkan ke atas SEMUA majikan mulai 1 Ogos 2025 (denda sehingga RM10,000 seorang pekerja) — tetapi gaji penengah sektor formal Johor hanya RM2,982 (Dis 2025), MASIH di bawah penengah nasional RM3,167, walaupun Johor ekonomi negeri paling pesat berkembang (KDNK +6.4% pada 2024).
issues.issue_en: The RM1,700 minimum wage took effect 1 February 2025 and became mandatory for ALL employers from 1 August 2025 (fines up to RM10,000 per worker) — yet Johor's formal-sector median wage is just RM2,982 (Dec 2025), STILL below the national median of RM3,167, even though Johor is Malaysia's fastest-growing state economy (GDP +6.4% in 2024).
issues.receipt_bm: The Star (18 Okt 2024): PM umum gaji minimum RM1,500→RM1,700 berkuat kuasa 1 Feb 2025 semasa membentang Belanjawan 2025. Malay Mail (22 Jul 2025): Kesuma — penguatkuasaan penuh seluruh negara mulai 1 Ogos 2025 tanpa penangguhan lanjut, denda sehingga RM10,000 bagi setiap pekerja terjejas. Business Today/DOSM (29 Apr 2026): gaji penengah sektor formal RM3,167 pada Dis 2025 (+4.0% setahun). The Edge (Jun 2026): penengah Johor RM2,982 — di bawah KL (RM4,391), Pulau Pinang (RM3,500) dan Selangor (RM3,400) — walaupun Johor catat pertumbuhan KDNK negeri terpantas 2024 (6.4%).
issues.receipt_en: The Star (18 Oct 2024): the PM announced the minimum wage rising RM1,500→RM1,700 from 1 Feb 2025 while tabling Budget 2025. Malay Mail (22 Jul 2025): Kesuma — full nationwide enforcement from 1 Aug 2025 with no further deferments, fines up to RM10,000 per affected worker. Business Today/DOSM (29 Apr 2026): formal-sector median wage RM3,167 in Dec 2025 (+4.0% YoY). The Edge (Jun 2026): Johor's median RM2,982 — below KL (RM4,391), Penang (RM3,500) and Selangor (RM3,400) — despite Johor posting 2024's fastest state GDP growth (6.4%).

issues.issue_bm: Rumah di Malaysia tergolong 'seriously unaffordable' (harga penengah 4.72 kali pendapatan tahunan, 2020) dan lonjakan JS-SEZ memburukkannya di Johor — harga apartmen servis JB melonjak 20.4% setahun (JLL, 2Q 2025); kerajaan janji sejuta rumah mampu milik menjelang 2035 dan tambah RM10 bilion jaminan pinjaman SJKP, tetapi tekanan harga dirasai SEKARANG.
issues.issue_en: Malaysian housing is 'seriously unaffordable' (median price 4.72 times annual income, 2020) and the JS-SEZ surge is making Johor worse — JB serviced-apartment prices jumped 20.4% in a year (JLL, 2Q 2025); the government pledges a million affordable homes by 2035 and an extra RM10 billion in SJKP loan guarantees, but the price pressure is being felt NOW.
issues.receipt_bm: DOSM/The Edge (2022): nisbah harga-pendapatan 4.72x pada 2020 — dalam kelompok 'seriously unaffordable' (4.1-5.0) kaedah Median Multiple. Malay Mail (31 Jul 2025): PM janji sejuta rumah mampu milik 2026-2035 bawah RMK-13. The Star (29 Okt 2025): tambahan RM10 bilion untuk Skim Jaminan Kredit Perumahan dalam Belanjawan 2026 (pembeli pertama tanpa slip gaji tetap, termasuk pekerja gig). The Malaysian Reserve/JLL (17 Jul 2025): harga transaksi purata apartmen servis JB naik 20.4% pada 2Q 2025 berbanding purata 2024; teres dua tingkat +8.6%. The Star (6 Dis 2025): 20,100 unit Rumah Mampu Milik Johor siap, 9,900 lagi dijangka 2026.
issues.receipt_en: DOSM/The Edge (2022): house price-to-income ratio 4.72x in 2020 — inside the 'seriously unaffordable' band (4.1-5.0) on the Median Multiple method. Malay Mail (31 Jul 2025): the PM pledged one million affordable homes 2026-2035 under the 13th Malaysia Plan. The Star (29 Oct 2025): an extra RM10 billion for the SJKP housing credit guarantee scheme in Budget 2026 (first-time buyers without fixed payslips, including gig workers). The Malaysian Reserve/JLL (17 Jul 2025): JB serviced-apartment average transaction prices +20.4% in 2Q 2025 vs the 2024 average; double-storey terraces +8.6%. The Star (6 Dec 2025): 20,100 Rumah Mampu Milik Johor units completed, 9,900 more due in 2026.
